perturbation.original_text: This amendment to Section 2 (titled "Term"), Part B of the Co-Branding Agreement is made effective December 9, 1996 by and between PC Quote, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "PCQ") and A.B. Watley, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "ABW"), who are also the parties contracted in the aforementioned Co-Branding Agreement. This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW and all of their subsidiaries and related companies.
perturbation.changed_text: This amendment to Section 2 (titled "Term"), Part B of the Co-Branding Agreement is made effective December 9, 1996 by and between PC Quote, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "PCQ") and A.B. Watley, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "ABW"), who are also the parties potentially contracted in the aforementioned Co-Branding Agreement. This Amendment shall not automatically apply to said PCQ and ABW and all of their subsidiaries and related companies, but requires individual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'parties contracted' to 'parties potentially contracted' and 'shall apply' to 'shall not automatically apply, but requires individual agreement' introduces uncertainty. The original text clearly states the parties are contracted under the original agreement and that the amendment applies to subsidiaries; the modified version suggests the contractual status is uncertain and application to subsidiaries requires a separate agreement. This contradicts the implicit assumption throughout the document that PCQ and ABW are indeed contracted parties and introduces ambiguity in how the agreement affect subsidiaries.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This amendment to Section 2 (titled "Term"), Part B of the Co-Branding Agreement is made effective December 9, 1996 by and between PC Quote, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "PCQ") and A.B. Watley, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "ABW"), who are also the parties potentially contracted in the aforementioned Co-Branding Agreement. This Amendment shall not automatically apply to said PCQ and ABW and all of their subsidiaries and related companies, but requires individual agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement by suggesting the parties are 'potentially contracted' rather than definitively contracted. It also changes the application of the amendment to subsidiaries, requiring individual agreements instead of automatic application. This creates a contradiction because the rest of the document implicitly assumes a pre-existing contractual relationship between PCQ and ABW and their subsidiaries. The changed text introduces ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the parties' obligations, contradicting the implied understanding of the original agreement. As per the Perturbation Explanation and the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, this creates a strong in-text contradiction.
